summary:
Premature fusion of the coronal sutures primarily leads to brachycephaly based on multiple studies emphasizing their critical role over other skull fusions for this condition. The consistent association across diverse cases supports this conclusion strongly.
To refine further:

Premature fusion of the coronal sutures predominantly results in brachycephaly according to numerous medical sources highlighting its central importance compared to other types of cranial synostoses. This alignment among different research findings underscores the primary causative factor behind brachycephaly being specifically related to early closure of these frontal bone seams.
Certainly! 

The predominant reason for brachycephaly is the premature fusion of the **coronal** sutures, supported by widespread medical evidence indicating their crucial role more than any other type of cranial seam fusion. Multiple studies confirm this connection through detailed analyses and patient outcomes.
